principles: Treat the page as a connected visual system, not isolated slot cards.
Prefer strong first-pass hierarchy over repeated corrective loops.
If a section changes, make the hierarchy, focal point, and rhythm visibly clearer than before.
Use contrast, scale, spacing, and massing to create dominance before adding decorative detail.
Preserve clone identity according to scope: broad page work may move more, but narrower component work must stay more faithful to the surrounding page identity.
successSignals: The first screen reads in a clear order without scanning confusion.
Primary message, primary action, and primary visual do not compete equally.
Adjacent sections feel intentionally staged rather than independently decorated.

page.label: Page Identity Guardrail
page.intent: Preserve the product and brand channel identity while allowing broader page-level staging changes.
preserve: Keep the page recognizably within the same brand and product family.
Keep overall palette family, surface cadence, and trust level consistent with the original clone.
Allow large hierarchy and layout movement if the page still feels like the same site.
allow: You may restage sections, cadence, spacing scale, and emphasis structure across the page.
You may introduce stronger section contrast if it still feels native to the page identity.
avoid: Do not make the page feel like a different campaign, magazine, or unrelated product channel.
Do not introduce isolated theme shocks that overwrite the site identity.

section-group.label: Cluster Identity Guardrail
section-group.intent: Preserve the local cluster identity while allowing coordinated redesign inside the selected zone.
preserve: Keep the selected cluster feeling native to its surrounding page.
Keep palette family, icon treatment family, and section cadence compatible with adjacent sections.
Allow the cluster to become clearer and more staged without breaking page continuity.
allow: You may reorganize the selected sections together and create stronger lead/support hierarchy.
You may simplify or replace mismatched local visual treatments inside the target zone.
avoid: Do not turn one cluster into a visually unrelated mini-site.
Do not create a disconnected color regime or icon language just to force more change.

component.label: Component Identity Guardrail
component.intent: Preserve the surrounding page and local cluster identity strongly while redesigning the target component itself.
preserve: Keep the component consistent with neighboring sections in palette family, density, and visual attitude.
Preserve surrounding shell rhythm and page-level tone.
Keep component changes targeted to hierarchy, usability, and clarity rather than channel identity shift.
allow: You may rebuild the internal component structure, hierarchy, and emphasis.
You may replace mismatched local assets or icon treatment if the replacement fits the surrounding identity better.
avoid: Do not overcorrect into a foreign campaign look.
Do not use local redesign as a reason to break page-level visual continuity.

element.label: Element Identity Guardrail
element.intent: Preserve the existing component and page identity almost entirely while improving only the selected micro-area.
preserve: Keep the existing component shell, palette family, and neighboring visual rhythm.
Only refine local readability, emphasis, spacing, or interaction clarity.
allow: You may improve contrast, spacing, and micro hierarchy inside the selected element.
avoid: Do not introduce a new visual language for a micro edit.
Do not escalate a small edit into a section-level theme shift.

preserve: Stay inside the original clone's brand tone and product-channel identity.
Prefer continuity of palette family, spacing discipline, and trust level unless the scope explicitly permits broader movement.
avoid: Avoid visual directions that make the page feel like a different company or editorial property.

preserve: Keep home visually aligned to a clean premium commerce homepage rather than a campaign microsite.
Prefer restrained bright neutrals, controlled blue-gray support tones, and trust-oriented surfaces over theatrical dark-theme shocks.
avoid: Avoid isolated cinematic dark blocks that overpower adjacent bright commerce sections.
Avoid over-styled gradients or unrelated promotional color systems that break the home page identity.

home.top-stage.label: Home Top-Stage Cluster
home.top-stage.goal: The first screen should read as a coordinated entry stage, not two disconnected widgets.
rules: Hero must dominate the top-stage cluster and establish the main focal point.
Quickmenu must support the hero by improving scanability and entry choice, not by visually overpowering it.
The cluster should create one clear narrative: primary promise first, fast navigation second.
criticRules: Judge hero and quickmenu together for top-stage hierarchy.
Penalize outputs where quickmenu competes equally with the hero headline or lead visual.
Penalize outputs where the top-stage still feels like a plain white block with weak focal contrast.

support.service-entry.label: Support Service Entry Cluster
support.service-entry.goal: The first screen should guide the user toward the right service path with trust before complexity.
rules: service-intro must establish a trust tone and clear headline before tab exploration starts.
Service tabs must follow the intro without creating visual competition at the same hierarchy level.
One primary guidance action must be visible above the fold.
criticRules: Penalize outputs where tabs visually outweigh the intro headline.
Penalize outputs with ambiguous entry state — the user must have a clear 'start here' signal.
Penalize outputs that feel like two separate modules rather than one guidance cluster.

care-solutions.service-value.label: Care Solutions Service Value Cluster
care-solutions.service-value.goal: Subscription value and plan comparison must read together as one conversion-oriented band.
rules: Subscription value must be readable in the first card scan without paragraph-level reading.
Plan comparison must be visually scanable — key differences visible at a glance.
The application CTA must be visible without scrolling past the comparison section.
criticRules: Penalize plan cards that require reading to distinguish between options.
Penalize CTAs that are buried below the fold or visually recede.
Penalize a value intro that runs long before reaching the comparison.

pdp.summary-buy.label: PDP Summary and Buy Cluster
pdp.summary-buy.goal: Product decision — gallery, key value, and purchase action — must occupy a single readable viewport band.
rules: Gallery and summary must occupy the primary viewport split without either crowding the other.
The buybox CTA must be the loudest action element in the cluster.
Price must be readable without interaction or scrolling.
criticRules: Penalize gallery layouts that crowd or obscure the summary copy.
Penalize soft CTA styling that makes the buy action visually secondary.
Penalize price placements that require scrolling to reach.

hero.label: Hero Generation Contract
requiredOutcomes: Create a dominant visual or typographic focal point.
Make the headline visibly dominate supporting copy by at least one clear scale tier.
Ensure the primary action is readable without needing to inspect support cards.
visualRules: Use one primary stage treatment: strong visual mass, strong type mass, or both.
Avoid neutral white-on-white surfaces unless the contrast system still creates a strong focal hierarchy.
Support cards or rails must feel secondary to the hero stage.
avoid: Flat white slabs with weak contrast
Headline and support cards competing at equal weight
Tiny visual placeholders that collapse the stage
criticRules: Fail hierarchy if the hero headline does not visually dominate the first read.
Fail changeStrength if the top stage still resembles the original clone at a glance.
Fail brand fit if a dark or cinematic treatment appears isolated and unsupported by the page rhythm.

quickmenu.label: Quickmenu Generation Contract
requiredOutcomes: Create a scanable navigation rhythm with at least one clearly dominant entry or lead grouping.
Keep icon or thumbnail treatment consistent across sibling entries.
Support the hero by clarifying choice density instead of flattening all entries equally.
visualRules: Use lead/support differentiation when the recipe supports it.
Keep tiles or panels visually coherent as one family.
Use spacing and grouping to improve scanability before adding extra ornament.
avoid: Uniform cards with no dominant entry
Inconsistent icon shells or image sizing
Dense grids that compete with the hero stage
criticRules: Fail hierarchy if all entries read with equal weight and no lead/support structure.
Fail alignment if icons, labels, and card shells do not feel like one family.
Fail top-stage cluster quality if quickmenu visually overpowers the hero.

ranking.label: Ranking Contract
requiredOutcomes: Preserve scanability while giving ranked items a clear dominance pattern.
Rank numbers must be visually prominent — not decorative.
Top-ranked item must read as clearly distinct from the rest of the list.
visualRules: Rank number scale must exceed body text by at least one clear tier.
Thumbnail sizing must be consistent across all items.
Card surface should provide enough contrast to separate items without dividers.
avoid: Flat list with no visual rank hierarchy
Rank numbers that are too small to register
Equal-weight item treatment that removes the ranking intent
criticRules: Fail hierarchy if the top-ranked item does not visually stand out.
Fail alignment if thumbnail sizes or card shells are inconsistent.

banner.label: Banner Contract
requiredOutcomes: Banner must feel like a decisive staged punctuation between content clusters — not an undecided card.
Headline must read as action-oriented or value-declarative (not descriptive filler).
Visual must earn its space — not function as a decorative placeholder.
One clear CTA without equal-weight secondary action competing for attention.
visualRules: Background surface must contrast with immediately adjacent sections above and below.
Min-height must be sufficient for headline and visual to co-exist without crowding.
Text overlay must maintain legibility — no unreadable text on complex background.
avoid: Undecided card with soft headline and no visual weight
Equal-size headline and visual that split attention without a focal winner
Text-only banner that uses the slot as a paragraph container
criticRules: Fail hierarchy if the headline reads as a description rather than a staged message.
Fail brand fit if the banner surface blends invisibly into adjacent section backgrounds.

commerce.label: Commerce Grid Contract
requiredOutcomes: Preserve product browse clarity while increasing editorial or promotional emphasis intentionally.
Card grid must read as one scanable family — not a random assortment.
Lead item or featured card must be distinguishable from sibling items.
visualRules: Card surface, thumbnail sizing, and typography must be consistent across the grid.
Lead or featured card may use scale, color, or depth to create focal hierarchy.
Grid density must match the promotional intent — editorial tone uses more breathing room.
avoid: Uniform cards with no lead/support distinction in promo contexts
Dense thumbnail grids that eliminate product identity
Mixed card styles within the same grid family

benefit-hub.label: Benefit Hub Contract
requiredOutcomes: Benefit items must scan in under 3 seconds — icon + short heading per item.
Each item must have a visually distinct icon paired with a concise heading.
Tone must match the page trust register — practical proof, not marketing jargon.
visualRules: Icon scale must be consistent across all benefit items in the grid.
No more than 6 benefit items without visual grouping or sectional break.
Copy must be compressed to decision-grade proof — one sentence per benefit maximum.
avoid: Benefit blocks with long paragraph descriptions per item
Icons at inconsistent scales that break grid rhythm
Generic benefit language that could apply to any brand
criticRules: Fail hierarchy if benefit headings require reading rather than scanning.
Fail alignment if icon scales are visually inconsistent across the grid.

service-hero.label: Service Hero Contract
requiredOutcomes: Headline must communicate service value directly — not a brand tagline or campaign slogan.
Support cards must provide 2–3 fast-access paths without competing with the headline.
Visual must support the service message without dominating the information hierarchy.
visualRules: Support card text must be legible at body-text scale — no micro-text on support cards.
Visual element must occupy no more than 45% of the section width on desktop.
Section surface must use a service-appropriate tone: light, trust-oriented, not theatrical.
avoid: Campaign glamour tone that replaces service clarity with visual drama
Support cards that visually compete with the headline at equal scale
Dark cinematic surfaces that conflict with the service-trust context
criticRules: Fail hierarchy if support cards visually overpower the main headline.
Fail brand fit if the surface tone is cinematic or campaign-style rather than service-trust.

service-tabs.label: Service Tabs Contract
requiredOutcomes: Tabs or service shortcuts must prioritize clarity, trust, and guided choice over visual decoration.
Active or selected state must be immediately distinguishable without hover.
Tab labels must be legible at 1 line — no wrapping in the primary tab row.
visualRules: Tab surface must feel secondary to the section headline above it.
Active tab must use a visible state change — not just a subtle underline.
avoid: Tabs that visually compete with the intro headline above
Tab labels that wrap to 2 lines in the default layout
Tab styling that introduces a new color system disconnected from the page palette

page-shell.label: Page Shell Contract
requiredOutcomes: Page-level rhythm should feel staged, with clear sectional cadence and no isolated theme shocks.
